sn35.115:1.1: “There are forms, monks, cognizable via the eye—agreeable, pleasing, charming, endearing, enticing, linked to sensual desire.
sn35.115:1.2: If a monk relishes them, welcomes them, & remains fastened to them,
sn35.115:1.3: he is said to be a monk fettered to forms cognizable by the eye. He has gone over to Māra’s camp; he has come under Māra’s power.
sn35.115:1.4: The Evil One can do with him as he will.
sn35.115:2.1: “There are flavors cognizable via the tongue…
sn35.115:2.2: “There are ideas cognizable via the intellect—agreeable, pleasing, charming, endearing, enticing, linked to sensual desire.
sn35.115:2.3: If a monk relishes them, welcomes them, & remains fastened to them,
sn35.115:2.4: he is said to be a monk fettered to ideas cognizable by the intellect. He has gone over to Māra’s camp; he has come under Māra’s power.
sn35.115:2.5: The Evil One can do with him as he will...
sn35.115:3.1: “Now, there are forms cognizable via the eye—agreeable, pleasing, charming, endearing, enticing, linked to sensual desire.
sn35.115:3.2: If a monk does not relish them, welcome them, or remain fastened to them,
sn35.115:3.3: he is said to be a monk freed from forms cognizable by the eye. He has not gone over to Māra’s camp; he has not come under Māra’s power.
sn35.115:3.4: The Evil One cannot do with him as he will...
sn35.115:4.1: “There are flavors cognizable via the tongue…
sn35.115:4.2: “There are ideas cognizable via the intellect—agreeable, pleasing, charming, endearing, enticing, linked to sensual desire.
sn35.115:4.3: If a monk does not relish them, welcome them, or remain fastened to them,
sn35.115:4.4: he is said to be a monk freed from ideas cognizable by the intellect. He has not gone over to Māra’s camp; he has not come under Māra’s power.
sn35.115:4.5: The Evil One cannot do with him as he will.”
